<!-- consumed by: .github/scripts/pretooluse-flutter-guard.sh -->
Stopping the Flutter dev server: `kill`/`kill -9`/`pkill` orphans the `frontend_server`/`dartaot` child process — orphaned compilers pile up, hold the `.dart_tool/` build lock, and starve CPU so the next run hangs. Stop it gracefully instead: `printf 'q' > /tmp/f8090`. If you're clearing an existing zombie pile, kill by process GROUP (`kill -- -$(ps -o pgid= -p "$pid" | tr -d ' ')`), never a bare PID. See run-flutter-web-local's Clean restart and Zombie recovery sections. And once the server is back up: open a FRESH tab rather than reloading the old one — a reload right after a restart races the dev-server boot and serves a stale bundle (or can wedge the connection); close the old tab only after the new one is driving.
